description:
GOAL: Replace the existing direct-style Cranelift codegen entirely. The old src/codegen.rs path goes away. jit::run_str, TieredRuntime, reverse thunks, on_user_call hook, tier-up policy — all rewired to use the CPS path.

## Decisions captured

- GREEN FIELD. No parallel paths, NO feature flag (user decision: "there is no 'old' — all of this is new"). The old codegen is deleted; tests adjust as needed.
- Tier-up still works conceptually: count interp calls per user_name; threshold trigger; JIT-compile that fn (now via CPS codegen) and rebind interp's globals to a Value::Jit pointing at the trampoline + initial frame template.
- Interp ↔ JIT marshaling: when interp calls a JIT'd fn, build initial frame, drive trampoline, extract result, marshal back to Value.
- Reverse thunks: still needed for the interp -> JIT call path. They now allocate the initial frame and call the trampoline.
- Value::Jit / Value::JitPoly stay; their fn_ptr now points at the CPS trampoline entry for that user_name.
- The on_user_call hook in eval.rs stays — it fires when the interp evaluates a Closure, where tier-up decisions are made.

## Deliverables

- Remove (or substantially rewrite) src/codegen.rs.
- Rewrite src/jit.rs's run_str / TieredRuntime / call_jit / call_jit_poly to use the new CPS codegen.
- Update reverse thunks for the new ABI.
- Existing test suite (~190 tests at this point in the arc) must continue to pass.

## Tests

- Re-run the entire JIT test suite under the new codegen.
- Tier-up still triggers correctly (existing tier_up_patches_interp_binding_after_threshold, etc.).
- count(100000, 0) still doesn't overflow (now via CPS trampoline + frame reuse).
- Polymorphic dispatch still works (poly_id_inspect_binding_after_tier_up).

## Risks / open

- Biggest single ticket of the arc by surface area. Touches tier-up + reverse thunks + JitCtx + on_user_call hook simultaneously.
- Strategy: feature-branch-style commit; we don't ship until everything works.
- Reverse thunk ABI change: the slot-buffer marshaling vs. frame allocation tradeoff. Probably keep the slot ABI at the boundary and have the JIT side allocate/populate the initial frame from slots.

## Notes for future-me picking this up

- on_user_call hook is in src/eval.rs:Interp::apply (the Closure arm).
- TieredRuntime lives in src/jit.rs. Its on_call / tier_up methods drive compilation.
- After this ticket, src/jit.rs may shrink considerably: lots of slot marshaling helpers (value_to_slots etc.) become unnecessary if frames are the unit of marshaling.
title: Cut over JIT entry points to CPS codegen (no legacy)
